note: seq=125: LIFELINE hardening 2/N. Upgrade the self-heal watchdog to v2: besides restarting a DOWN Postgres or a DEAD worker, it now detects a worker that is ACTIVE but PRODUCING NOTHING (CPU ~0 over a 15s sample while there is work) -> restarts it. This is the deeper lesson from today: nothing is assumed healthy just because the process is 'running'. Loud logging (file + journal via logger) on every heal so other agents/the daily watchdog surface it. Overwrites /opt/atlas/atlas_pg_selfheal.sh; the 60s timer already runs it.

steps.note: seq=125: producing-zero detection + loud heal logging.

steps.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# ATLAS lifeline self-heal v2: PG liveness + worker liveness + worker PRODUCING(cpu) + loud logging
LOG=/var/log/atlas-selfheal.log
ts(){ date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ; }
alert(){ echo "$(ts) $*" >> "$LOG"; logger -t atlas-selfheal "$*" 2>/dev/null; }
WORKERS="atlas-enrich.service atlas-enrich-async-ramp.service atlas-async-ramp.service"
if ! pg_isready -h 127.0.0.1 -p 5432 -t 5 >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  alert "CRITICAL postgres not answering -> restart postgresql"
  systemctl restart postgresql; sleep 8
fi
cpu_ns(){ local v; v=$(systemctl show -p CPUUsageNSec --value "$1" 2>/dev/null); echo "${v//[!0-9]/}"; }
for u in $WORKERS; do
  systemctl list-unit-files | grep -q "^$u" || continue
  if ! systemctl is-active --quiet "$u"; then
    alert "$u inactive -> restart"; systemctl reset-failed "$u" 2>/dev/null; systemctl restart "$u"; continue
  fi
  c1=$(cpu_ns "$u"); [ -z "$c1" ] && c1=0
  sleep 15
  c2=$(cpu_ns "$u"); [ -z "$c2" ] && c2=0
  delta=$(( c2 - c1 ))
  if [ "$delta" -lt 100000000 ]; then
    alert "$u ACTIVE but producing nothing (cpu ${delta}ns/15s) -> restart (hung-but-running)"
    systemctl reset-failed "$u" 2>/dev/null; systemctl restart "$u"
  fi
done
